title: `quiver:blame-slice` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver blame-slice <file>` that lists every slice whose `files[]` declared the given path, along with the PR number and merge date from git history.
description: `git blame` tells you who changed a line; it doesn't tell you which slice was responsible. This command bridges the gap: given a file path, it scans every `slice.json.files` to find matching slices, then uses `git log --follow <file>` to locate the merge commit for each slice's branch and extract the PR number from the commit message.
must: Scan every `slice.json` for the given file in `files[]`
Use `git log --follow --format='%H|%s|%ci' -- <file>` to locate merge commits
Parse PR numbers from commit messages via regex `#(\d+)`
Print: slice id, ticket, PR number (if detected), merge date
Add a section to `docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` titled `Finding the slice that introduced a regression` documenting `git bisect run`
Add a `docs/COMMANDS.md` row and update `README.md` npm scripts list
not_included: Querying GitHub API for PR details (only text in commit messages)
acceptance: `npx create-quiver blame-slice src/create-quiver/lib/init-docs.js` returns at least one slice for an existing file with slice history
For a file with no matching slice, exit 0 with message `No slice references this file`
`--json` output is an array of `{ slice, ticket, pr, merge_date }`
`docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` has a new section covering `git bisect run`
assumptions: PR numbers appear in merge commit messages as `#N`
`git log --follow` is reliable enough for the path renames we care about
Users don't need line-level blame from this command
